QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_16

question.text: SEBI was established under which Act of Parliament?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI was established under Section 3 of the SEBI Act, 1992 as a statutory body. Its primary role is to protect investor interests, promote development of securities market, and regulate the securities market. **The Trap:** SCRA 1956 existed before SEBI and governs securities contracts and stock exchange recognition. SEBI was created later to provide a dedicated regulatory body. **Cross-Exam Context:** Virtually all NISM exams test SEBI's establishment and role. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Four pillars of securities regulation: (1) SEBI Act 1992 — regulator establishment, (2) SCRA 1956 — stock exchange and contracts, (3) Depositories Act 1996 — electronic holding, (4) Companies Act 2013 — corporate governance. SEBI administers all four.
